title: webtrigger onFocus
text:
webtrigger onFocus This web trigger is used to program behavior when the DSP widget gets focus as a result of user interaction. Declaration: Explain this code webtriggeronFocus Optional scope block Optional variables block javascript Javascript to implement the client-side trigger endjavascript end Copy Parameters: None Applies to: This trigger is applicable only on HTML-based DSP widgets that can get and lose focus. htmlinput htmlselect htmlradiogroup htmltextarea genericHTML Activation: Activated when the widget receives focus by the user tabbing to, or clicking on, a field in a DSP. Default behavior: None Behavior upon completion: None Description If defined, the onFocus trigger is fired when the widget receives focus, by the user tabbing to or clicking on a field in a DSP. The onBlur trigger can be used to reverse the effects caused by the onFocus trigger. Example: Using onFocus In the following example, a web trigger displays a
element as a popup when the field gets the focus. A HTML
element is defined in the layout as:
Use uppercase letters only.
Copy The following onFocus trigger displays the element: webtrigger onfocus scope output endscope javascript document.getElementById('popover').style.visibility = 'visible'; endjavascript end Copy The effect can be reversed in the onBlur trigger: webtrigger OnBlur javascript document.getElementById('popover').style.visibility = 'hidden'; endjavascript end Copy Related Links webtrigger onBlur This web trigger is used to program behavior when the DSP widget loses focus.
